weeks.title: Subtle Bodies
weeks.tradition_focus: YTT kosha system; foundational MMK; AKBh dharma-ontology.

items.label: YTT Manual: Section 1 — Kosha System, Chakras, Nāḍīs, Prāṇa, Aura
Satchidananda, Yoga Sūtras: 1.1–1.11; 1.17–1.19; 1.41
Iyengar, Light on Yoga: Introduction ("The whole man"); prāṇāyāma intro
Module B Wk 1: MMK Preface \+ Intro (Siderits & Katsura); VV Introduction (Westerhoff); GSN Ch. 1
Module C Wk 1: AKBh Ch. 1, I.1–5 (Sangpo Vol. 1\)
Module A Wk 1: Smith, Yoga of the Natural State — Translator's Preface \+ opening orientation to gzhi
Kṣemarāja, Pratyabhijñāhṛdayam sūtras 1–4 (Singh)
*Subtle-body baseline
Śamatha foundation.

weeks.title: Subtle Bodies, continued
weeks.tradition_focus: Causation and motion (MMK); rūpa-skandha (AKBh); Kunjed Gyalpo orientation.

items.label: YTT Manual: Section 1 (finish) — Annamaya/Prāṇamaya; Manomaya/Vijñānamaya; Aura
Module B Wk 2: MMK Ch. 1 (Analysis of Conditions); MMK Ch. 2 (Analysis of Motion)
Module C Wk 2: AKBh Ch. 1, I.6–28 (rūpa-skandha, four primary elements, eight-substance molecule); I.29–47 (eighteen dhātus enumeration — skim structure)
Module A Wk 2: Smith, YNS — Kunjed Gyalpo section; gzhi as ground of all appearances
\[\[D-integration\]\] Tenzin Wangyal, Awakening the Sacred Body — introductory chapters on Bön/Dzogchen subtle body (nine breathings, tsa-lung)
*Causation in the prāṇa-claim
Continue śamatha; add brief analytical sit on "where does the breath come from?" (MMK 1 applied somatically).

weeks.title: Ayurveda
weeks.tradition_focus: Guṇas, tattvas, doṣas; āyatanas/skandhas/dhātus; Leick three-doors investigation.

items.label: YTT Manual: Section 2 — Vedas, Guṇas, Tattvas, Tridoṣic System, Disease Process
Satchidananda, YS: 2.1–2.9 (kriyā yoga, kleśas, avidyā); 2.18–2.25 (the seen and the Seer; three guṇas)
Iyengar, LoY: review eight-limbs material with attention to the body's relationship to constitution
Module B Wk 3: MMK Ch. 3–5 (Āyatanas, Skandhas, Dhātus); Letter to a Friend — opening verses on six sense-spheres
Module C Wk 3: AKBh Ch. 1, I.5–15 (five skandhas formally defined); three unconditioned dharmas; conditioned characteristics
Module A Wk 3: Smith, YNS — short aural lineage I
\[\[D-integration\]\] Tenzin Wangyal, Healing with Form, Energy, and Light — five elements as outer/inner phenomena (chapters covering threefold path)
Leick, "The Way to Understand Nature and Oneself"
*Three-doors vs
Brief daily three-doors investigation (body / energy / mind, as Leick demonstrates).

weeks.title: Yoga Nidra
weeks.tradition_focus: Four states, *turīya*, witness-self, *saṅkalpa*, iRest; *bsam pa* applied to consciousness states.

items.label: YTT Manual: Section 3 — States of Consciousness, Turīya, Saṅkalpa, Self as Witness, iRest
Satchidananda, YS: 1.17–1.51 (samādhi stages through nirbīja); 3.1–3.11 (dhāraṇā/dhyāna/samādhi)
Iyengar, LoY: prāṇāyāma intro \+ technique sections (the bridge from gross to subtle)
Module B Wk 4: MMK Ch. 6–9 (relational identity; agent/action; saṃsāra; the composite)
Module C Wk 4: AKBh Ch. 2 (saṃskāra; citta; mahābhūmika mental factors — Mental Factors selections)
Module A Wk 4: Smith, YNS — short aural lineage II; rang rig introduction
Kṣemarāja, Pratyabhijñāhṛdayam sūtra 18 (turīya and turyātīta — the strongest defense of witness-self)
\[\[D-integration\]\] Satyananda, Yoga Nidrā — introductory chapters; four-level consciousness map; rotation-of-consciousness protocol
Berkovich-Ohana, "Meditation, Psychedelics and the Self"
*Three accounts of turīya
Śamatha-vipaśyanā integration; one Yoga Nidra session this week observed analytically (notice when the cueing language imports a witness-self, and what your mind does with it).

weeks.title: Advanced Anatomy & Physiology
weeks.tradition_focus: Subtle ↔ gross interaction; nervous system; fascia; prāṇāyāma anatomy; bardo material compressed from Module A.

items.label: YTT Manual: Section 4 — Subtle Energy \+ Physical Body Interaction, Nervous System, Fascia, Prāṇāyāma Anatomy
Satchidananda, YS: 3.26–3.48 (saṃyama on the body and its elements)
Iyengar, LoY: āsana intro; 5–6 specific āsana descriptions from your current teaching practice (read for implicit assumptions about the practitioner-self)
Module B Wk 5: MMK Ch. 10–12 (Fire/Fuel; Saṃsāra; Suffering)
Module C Wk 5: AKBh Ch. 2 — cittaviprayukta (factors dissociated from mind)
Module A Wks 5–6 compressed: Smith, YNS — middle-length aural lineage (bardo material). This is the only compression point in Module A; give it real attention.
Kṣemarāja, Pratyabhijñāhṛdayam sūtra 14 (fivefold activity of citi)
\[\[D-integration\]\] Anand, Neuroscience of Yoga Part 1 — autonomic regulation, HPA-axis, vagal tone
\[\[D-integration\]\] Murphy & Brewer, Interoception: A Comprehensive Guide — introductory chapters defining interoception
*The fascia-as-prāṇa-medium audit
Add interoceptive precision to your daily practice: name temperature, pressure, density rather than "energy" or "prāṇa." Begin Cue Log entries explicitly testing replacement cues.

weeks.title: Advanced Sequencing
weeks.tradition_focus: Sequencing as dependent origination; peak-pose logic; pedagogical theory.

items.label: YTT Manual: Section 5 — Sequencing Logic, Āsana Architecture, Mind-Body in Āsana, Chakra-Informed Sequencing
Satchidananda, YS: 1.12–1.16 (abhyāsa and vairāgya); 2.46–2.53 (āsana, prāṇāyāma, pratyāhāra)
Iyengar, LoY: āsana classification sections (forward bends, back bends, twists, inversions); at least one complete standing pose set
Module B Wk 6: MMK Ch. 13–15 (Conjunction, the Composite, Svabhāva — the heart of the emptiness argument)
Module C Wk 6: AKBh Ch. 3 (loka — sentient beings and their world; six hetus \+ four pratyayas as causal framework)
Module A Wk 7: Smith, YNS — middle-length aural lineage II
Kṣemarāja, Pratyabhijñāhṛdayam sūtra 8 (unmeṣa / nimeṣa; spanda)
\[\[D-integration\]\] Betsy Ng (ed.), Self-Determination Theory and Socioemotional Learning — autonomy-supportive teaching chapters
*A 6-pose sequence built on dependent origination
Teach this sequence (or rehearse it solo if no class).

weeks.title: Prenatal Yoga
weeks.tradition_focus: Ethics of interdependence; ratnāvalī axis opens; AKBh karma; Lifshitz/Veissière threading begins.

items.label: YTT Manual: Section 6 — Female Anatomy, Reproductive Cycle, Developing Fetus, Fourth Trimester
Satchidananda, YS: 2.30–2.45 (yamas \+ niyamas in detail); special attention to ahiṃsā and aparigraha
Iyengar, LoY: sections on women's health āsanas / supported inversions (Sarvāṅgāsana family)
Module B Wk 7: MMK Ch. 16–17 (Bondage and Liberation; Examination of Action) — opens Phase III
Module C Wk 7: AKBh Ch. 4 (karma — opens the ethics module)
Module A Wk 8: Smith, YNS — great aural lineage I
Kṣemarāja, Pratyabhijñāhṛdayam sūtra 13 (cidānanda-lābha)
Lifshitz & Veissière, "Cultural Neurophenomenology of Hypnosis and Meditation"
*The self-other boundary in prenatal teaching
Continue śamatha-vipaśyanā.

weeks.title: Restorative Yoga
weeks.tradition_focus: *Praśrabdhi* (suppleness); CNS regulation; "surrender" and its theological commitments.

items.label: YTT Manual: Section 7 — CNS Regulation, Nervous System, Props, Therapeutic Sequencing
Satchidananda, YS: 1.30–1.39 (obstacles \+ īśvarapraṇidhāna as antidote); 2.46–2.48 (āsana as sthira-sukha)
Iyengar, LoY: Śavāsana and Viparīta Karaṇī in detail
Module B Wk 8: MMK Ch. 18–20 (Self, Time, Combination — Phase II close)
Module C Wk 8: AKBh Ch. 5 (anuśaya / latent dispositions — the kleśa map begins)
Module A Wk 9: Smith, YNS — great aural lineage II
Kṣemarāja, Pratyabhijñāhṛdayam sūtra 19 (samāveśa; cittapralaya)
\[\[D-integration\]\] Chögyal Namkhai Norbu, Teachers' Training — discussion of teacher's responsibility in pointing-out moments (opening chapters)
Kirkland, "Harnessing the Power of Physical Activity for Mental Health"
*"Surrender" without ātman
Longer restorative session this week observed as condition-creation rather than rest-attainment.

weeks.title: Yin Yoga
weeks.tradition_focus: Daoism, yin-yang, meridians; two truths; *abhyāsa-vairāgya* as the more rigorous polarity.

items.label: YTT Manual: Section 8 — Daoism, Yin-Yang, Meridians, Fascia, Long Holds
Satchidananda, YS: 1.12–1.16 (revisit abhyāsa \+ vairāgya with yin-yang in mind)
Iyengar, LoY: forward-bend descriptions (Paścimottānāsana family) — read for what sustained passive stretching is described as doing
Module B Wk 9: MMK Ch. 21–23 (Arising and Ceasing; Tathāgata; False Conceptions — Phase III work)
Module C Wk 9: AKBh Ch. 5 (kleśa map continued)
Module A Wk 10: Smith, YNS — Great Guide; six lamps introduction
Kṣemarāja, Pratyabhijñāhṛdayam sūtra 8 (revisit spanda)
*Three frameworks for a Yin class
One long-hold session this week (3–10 min per pose) as temporal phenomenology — observe the moment-to-moment shifts in sensation, thought, attention.

weeks.title: Trauma-Informed Yoga
weeks.tradition_focus: Suffering as process, not possession; the *kleśa* map; Capriles on Four Noble Truths of Ecology. **The heaviest listening week of the semester — plan accordingly.**

items.label: YTT Manual: Section 9 — Somatic Experiencing, ANS, Identity, Separation, Inner Critic
Satchidananda, YS: 2.1–2.17 (kleśas \+ saṃskāras); 2.33–2.34 (pratipakṣa bhāvanā)
Iyengar, LoY: prāṇāyāma chapter — psychological effects sections
Module B Wk 10: MMK Ch. 24 (Examination of the Four Noble Truths — the prapañcopaśama / dependent-origination verse)
Module C Wk 10: AKBh Ch. 5 continued (full kleśa exhaustive taxonomy)
Module A Wk 11: Smith, YNS — six lamps detailed
\[\[D-integration\]\] Anand, Neuroscience of Yoga Part 1 — trauma-relevant chapters (vagal tone, HPA, documented PTSD findings)
Capriles, "The Four Noble Truths of Ecology"
If pacing won't accommodate both, split Capriles across Weeks 10 and 12 rather than displace him. The MMK 24 \+ Capriles convergence is too clean to give up.
*Trauma as conditioned pattern (not stored object)
Honest evaluation of your own practice in difficult conditions this week.

weeks.title: Adaptive Yoga (M10) \+ M11 Business deep-dive lands
weeks.tradition_focus: *Upāya-kauśalya*; the path adapted to capacity; M11 Business / Ethics receives its full philosophical analysis this week (per Module D structure).

items.label: YTT Manual: Section 10 — Adaptive Populations, Modification, Inclusion, Chair Yoga
YTT Manual: Section 11 — Business of Yoga (full read this week; module material was threaded earlier but the deep analysis lands here)
Satchidananda, YS: 1.20–1.22 (three levels of intensity); 2.30–2.39 revisited (yamas, esp. aparigraha)
Iyengar, LoY: prop-system sections; preface (Krishnamacharya relationship)
Module B Wk 11: MMK Ch. 25–27 (Nirvāṇa; Twelve-link chain; Views — closing of MMK)
Module C Wk 11: AKBh Ch. 5 close \+ bridge to Ch. 6 (mārga survey)
Module A Wks 11–12 begin: Smith, YNS — four thögal visions; Ultimate Mirror
Kṣemarāja, Pratyabhijñāhṛdayam sūtra 19 (revisit — Trika tripartite upāyas)
\[\[D-integration\]\] Norbu, Teachers' Training — full priority pick: opening chapters on authentic teacher qualities \+ relational discipline of transmission
\[\[D-integration\]\] Tien-Feng Lee, Buddhist Ethics for Laypeople — chapters on right speech, right livelihood
\[\[D-integration\]\] Nicholson, Buddhism, Cognitive Science, and the Doctrine of Selflessness — selective for studio-translation language
Lifshitz/Veissière Pt. 3
Adaptive teaching without a normative body
Cue Log working entry — the brand question
Adaptive variations explored in your own sit; honest evaluation of how your practice serves (or doesn't) the breadth of bodies you encounter.
Module D Appendix B (Central Philosophical Tensions Tracker) — full read-through with your semester's journal entries on each tension
Re-read your Week 1, Week 4, Week 7 written deliverables (the tension anchors)
Smith on the gzhi / Nyingma-Madhyamaka synthesis (re-read of YNS introduction)
Kṣemarāja PH sūtras 1–4 and 12 (counterpoint anchors)

Vasubandhu AKBh IX selections on the refutation of the self (the Pudgala-pratiṣedha; this is preview material — the chapter itself is not formally on the Module C 15-week scope, but selections frame Week 12's tensions work)
Module B Wk 12: Candrakīrti Madhyamakāvatāra Ch. 1–6 (first six perfections; bodhisattva grounds)
Module C Wk 12: AKBh Ch. 6 (mārga — the path), Vol. 2 close. (Note: the recalibrated Module C ends with Ch. 5; mārga is preview-only via the Series II overview class.)
Module A: four thögal visions continued
Capriles re-listen on the sākṣin critique (the "false non-duality" formulation) — Running Tension \#1 anchor
Berkovich-Ohana re-listen on self-pattern theory — Running Tension \#1 anchor
The Tensions Document (Week 12 deliverable)
Sitting prompts (one per tension, from Module D Week 12):
Your Cue Log — fourteen weeks of entries, chronological order
Norbu, Teachers' Training — sections on view / conduct / speech (the audit framework)
Module B Wk 13: Candrakīrti MA Ch. 6–11; Śāntarakṣita Madhyamakālaṃkāra (Yogācāra-Madhyamaka synthesis as model for holding two views together)
Module C Wk 13: Mādhyamika critique of Abhidharma (transition material; AKBh IX selections on the refutation of the self where Module C bridges to Module B's Phase IV)
Module A: integration / synthesis of the four visions
uncertain
Practice from view: test the surviving cues in studio.
Your Tensions Document (Week 12\)
Your Working Cue Inventory (Week 13\)
Smith, YNS — re-read of the Introduction
Norbu, Teachers' Training — final chapters on integration of teaching and practice
Module B Wk 14: Nāgārjuna Ratnāvalī survey (esp. chapters on teaching, transmission, teacher-student); second-semester orientation
Module C Wk 14: bridge / Mādhyamika critique of Abhidharma; orientation toward the deferred Vols 3–4 module
Module A Wks 11–12 close compressed: final YNS synthesis of the four visions
The Teaching Philosophy Statement (Week 14 deliverable)
Whatever practice has emerged as foundational over the semester.
